title: Lumina Domain Authority Track Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for the domain authority parallel track. Domain authorities are NOT system-level roles — they operate on a separate authentication track with their own signing secret. See docs/7-concepts/parallel-authority-tracks.md.

role_id.description: The sole role on the domain track.

track.description: Authority track identifier. Always 'domain' for this schema.

description.description: Purpose and responsibilities of the domain authority role.

default_module_mode.description: Default octal mode when this DA owns a module.

scope_type.description: Access scope is always bounded by the governed_modules JWT claim.

token_scope.description: Token scope claim value for domain-track tokens.

max_session_scope.description: Domain authorities can view sessions only in their governed modules.

audit_visibility.description: Domain authorities can inspect audit trails only for their governed modules.

may_create_users.description: Whether this role can invite new users (limited to user/guest roles within governed modules).

may_assign_roles.description: Domain authorities cannot assign system-level roles. They can assign domain roles within their modules.

may_assign_domain_roles.description: Whether this role can assign domain-scoped roles within governed modules.

bypass_permissions.description: Domain authorities never bypass permission checks. They resolve as owner within governed modules.
